name: Tiny Tacos
instructions:
1. Add the Olive Oil, Onion, and Ground Beef to a cast iron skillet set over medium-high heat. Add the Salt, Chili Powder, Cumin, Chipotle Pepper, Garlic Powder, Black Pepper, Dried Oregano, and Cayenne Pepper. Break the beef apart with a spatula to mix well with the spices; cook until browned and crumbled into very small pieces, 5 to 7 minutes. The meat should be fully cooked and fragrant with spices.

2. Stir in the Tomato Paste and Water; bring to a simmer. Reduce heat to medium-low and cook until most of the liquid has reduced and the mixture becomes thick, 15 to 20 minutes. Stir in the Jalapeño peppers and cook for 1 minute to release their flavors. Turn off the heat, taste, and adjust seasoning to your preference.

3. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Stack up 2 or 3 Tortillas at a time and use a round pastry cutter to cut small circles out of each stack. You will get several small circles from each tortilla.

4. Mix the Monterey Jack and Cheddar cheeses together. Top each tortilla circle with a small amount of cheese, then add about a teaspoon of taco beef and another teaspoon of cheese on top. Arrange on a baking sheet.

5. Bake in the preheated oven until the cheese melts and becomes slightly bubbly, about 3 minutes. Remove from the oven and carefully fold each tortilla in half while still warm and pliable, forming tiny tacos.

6. Heat the Vegetable Oil in a nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Pan-fry the tacos until crispy and golden brown on both sides, about 2 minutes per side. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to absorb excess oil before serving.
simpleInstructions:
1. Cook the Ground Beef with Onion and all spices until browned and crumbled.

2. Add the Tomato Paste and Water; simmer until thickened.

3. Cut small circles from Tortillas, top with mixed cheese, beef, and more cheese.

4. Bake until cheese melts.

5. Fold the tortillas in half and fry until crispy.
